content.name: SoulBot Chat v1.0.0 — User Profiler
content.summary: Fractal user cognition engine. Detects emotions with cause extraction, tracks mood trajectories with causal patterns, learns preferences and habits, supports direct profile editing, tracks satisfaction and adaptation effectiveness, provides values-based decision support data, feedback correction storage with decay, and builds comprehensive user cognitive map.
content.description: Sub-AISOP of soulbot_chat (Pattern E fractal). Called by main.aisop.json for user understanding. Performs fractal analysis of user behavior — detecting emotions, learning preferences, recognizing habits, and building a comprehensive cognitive map of the user. Four operations: analyze_turn (real-time per-turn profiling), query_profile (read profile), generate_report (human-readable summary), reset_profile (data deletion). Privacy-preserving: only store what is necessary, respect data minimization. All analysis is probabilistic, never definitive. Token estimate: ~1000-2000 tokens per analysis (emotion detection + signal extraction + profile file_system I/O). Circuit-breaker default: 3 failures in 60s window.

operation.agentic_prompt: Profile operation: analyze_turn, query_profile, generate_report, reset_profile, edit_profile

user_id.agentic_prompt: Unique user identifier for profile storage and retrieval.

memory_dir.agentic_prompt: Base directory for memory storage. Profile files stored under {memory_dir}/profiles/.

edit_request.agentic_prompt: Profile edit request: {field_path: string, new_value: string|object, operation: 'set'|'remove', source: 'user_explicit'}

content.system_prompt:
User cognition analyst. Detect patterns with humility — never overclaim understanding. Respect privacy and autonomy. Analysis is probabilistic, not definitive. Mirror User's exact language and script variant.
Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit.

aisop.main:
graph TD
    Start[Profiler Input] --> OperationGate{Route by Operation}
    OperationGate -- analyze_turn --> DetectEmotion[Detect Emotion]
    OperationGate -- query_profile --> LoadProfile[Load Profile]
    OperationGate -- generate_report --> GenerateReport[Generate Report]
    OperationGate -- reset_profile --> ConfirmReset[Confirm Reset]
    OperationGate -- edit_profile --> EditProfile[Apply Profile Edit]
    DetectEmotion --> TrackMood[Track Mood Trajectory]
    TrackMood --> ExtractSignals[Extract Behavioral Signals]
    ExtractSignals --> InteractionMonitor[Monitor Interaction Quality]
    InteractionMonitor --> UpdateProfile[Update Profile]
    UpdateProfile --> endNode((End))
    LoadProfile --> endNode
    GenerateReport --> endNode
    ConfirmReset --> ClearProfile[Clear Profile Data]
    ClearProfile --> endNode
    EditProfile --> endNode

Start.step1: Receive operation, user_id, turn_data, conversation_history, and memory_dir from main router. Validate all inputs before routing.
Start.constraints: (A) Type guard — operation must be string, user_id must be string, turn_data must be object (when provided), conversation_history must be array (when provided), edit_request must be object with field_path:string and new_value (when provided). (B) Size guard — reject user_id > 128 chars, turn_data.user_input > 10000 chars, conversation_history > 50 turns. (C) Injection guard — strip shell metacharacters from user_id, reject user_id containing path separators or special characters beyond alphanumeric, hyphen, underscore. (D) Path guard — reject '..' in memory_dir; restrict all file operations to {memory_dir}/profiles/ and {memory_dir}/semantic/ subdirectories (profiles for profile data, semantic for relationship and fact data cleared during reset). (E) Encoding guard — NFC normalize all string inputs.
Start.Error: Invalid input -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return empty profile with error flag -> inform caller.

OperationGate.step1: Route by operation parameter: analyze_turn -> DetectEmotion (requires turn_data). query_profile -> LoadProfile. generate_report -> GenerateReport (step1 loads profile internally). reset_profile -> ConfirmReset. edit_profile -> EditProfile (requires edit_request).
OperationGate.step2: Validate operation-specific required fields: analyze_turn requires turn_data with at least user_input and timestamp. query_profile requires only user_id. generate_report requires only user_id. reset_profile requires only user_id. edit_profile requires user_id and edit_request with at least field_path and new_value.
OperationGate.constraints: Exactly one route. Unknown operation -> reject with supported operations list. Missing required fields for chosen operation -> inform caller which fields are needed.
OperationGate.Error: Unknown operation -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return supported operations list -> inform caller.

DetectEmotion.step1: Analyze turn_data.user_input to classify primary emotion from: joy, sadness, anger, fear, surprise, disgust, neutral, mixed. Consider conversation_history for context — a 'fine' after distressing turns may mask sadness. Assign intensity: low (subtle hint), medium (clear signal), high (dominant tone).
DetectEmotion.step2: Calculate confidence score (0.0-1.0): high confidence for explicit emotional language ('I'm so happy'), lower for ambiguous text ('okay'). Identify secondary_emotion when mixed signals present — e.g., primary: anger, secondary: sadness for frustrated grief.
DetectEmotion.step3: CAUSE EXTRACTION: parse user_input for causal language patterns indicating WHY the user feels the detected emotion. Patterns: explicit causation ('because', 'due to', 'since', 'after', 'when', 'caused by', '因为', '由于', '自从'), attribution markers ('he/she/they made me', 'it is my fault', '都是...的错'), temporal trigger ('ever since', 'after what happened', '从那以后'). Extract cause_context: { cause_topic: string|null (the subject causing the emotion, e.g., 'work deadline', 'argument with partner'), attribution_type: 'external_person'|'external_situation'|'self'|'unknown'|null, causal_confidence: float(0-1) }. LOW confidence (< 0.4) -> set cause_topic to null rather than guess. Short inputs (< 8 words) -> skip cause extraction, set cause_context to null. Include cause_context in output alongside emotion classification.
DetectEmotion.step4: Output: { emotion: string, intensity: 'low'|'medium'|'high', confidence: float, secondary_emotion: string|null, reasoning_hint: string, cause_context: { cause_topic: string|null, attribution_type: string|null, causal_confidence: float }|null }. cause_context is from step3 cause extraction. The reasoning_hint is internal only — a brief note on what triggered the classification for audit purposes. Include emotion_display: { label: string (human-readable emotion name, e.g., 'Feeling sad'), icon: string (emoji shortcode, e.g., ':pensive:'), color: string (CSS color for UI rendering, e.g., '#4A90D9' for sadness, '#4CAF50' for joy, '#F44336' for anger, '#FFC107' for surprise, '#9E9E9E' for neutral) }. This structured field enables consistent UI rendering across different display contexts.
DetectEmotion.constraints: NEVER present emotion detection as absolute truth — always probabilistic. Short inputs (< 5 words) -> default confidence cap at 0.5. Sarcasm and cultural context may confuse detection — note low confidence when ambiguity detected. Do NOT diagnose mental health conditions. Emotion classification is observational, not clinical.
DetectEmotion.Error: Classification fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return { emotion: 'neutral', intensity: 'low', confidence: 0.1, secondary_emotion: null } -> inform caller of fallback.

TrackMood.step1: Read existing mood trajectory from profile (via {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json). Append new emotion data point: { emotion, intensity, confidence, timestamp, cause_topic: cause_context.cause_topic|null, attribution_type: cause_context.attribution_type|null }. Maintain rolling window of last 50 data points for trajectory analysis.
TrackMood.step2: Detect trajectory patterns: (a) ESCALATING NEGATIVE — 3+ consecutive negative emotions (sadness, anger, fear, disgust) with non-decreasing intensity -> flag safety_concern: true. (b) SUSTAINED POSITIVE — 5+ consecutive joy/surprise with medium+ intensity -> note engagement_peak: true. (c) RAPID SHIFTS — emotion category changes 3+ times in 5 turns -> flag volatility: true. (d) BASELINE DRIFT — compare recent 10 entries against historical baseline, note if user's typical emotional register has shifted.
TrackMood.step2_5: CAUSE PATTERN DETECTION: analyze cause_topics across emotion_history (last 20 entries with non-null cause_topic). Identify: (a) RECURRING_CAUSE — same cause_topic in 3+ negative emotion entries -> flag { topic, frequency, avg_valence }. (b) ATTRIBUTION_SKEW — attribution_type consistently 'self' for 5+ negative entries -> flag self_blame_pattern: true. (c) ESCALATING_STRESSOR — same external cause_topic with increasing intensity trend -> flag { topic, intensity_trend: 'escalating' }. Pass detected patterns to UpdateProfile for storage in cognitive_map.cause_correlations.
TrackMood.step3: Calculate mood_baseline: weighted average of recent emotions, with newer entries weighted 2x. Represent as { valence: float(-1 to 1), arousal: float(0 to 1), stability: float(0 to 1) }.
TrackMood.constraints: Safety flags are informational — passed to main router for safety module handling, NOT acted upon by profiler. Trajectory analysis requires minimum 3 data points; fewer -> skip pattern detection. Do NOT make clinical interpretations of mood patterns.
TrackMood.Error: Trajectory analysis fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: append data point without pattern analysis, continue to ExtractSignals -> inform caller of partial analysis.

ExtractSignals.step1: From turn_data extract topic preferences: identify subjects the user discusses (technology, relationships, work, health, hobbies, current events, personal growth, etc.). Use lightweight keyword and semantic analysis — do NOT over-analyze single turns. Weight: explicit statements ('I love cooking') > implicit signals (asking about recipes). Track topic frequency and recency.
ExtractSignals.step2: Extract communication style signals: (a) Formality — formal markers (complete sentences, honorifics) vs casual markers (abbreviations, slang, emoji descriptions). (b) Verbosity — message length relative to content density. (c) Directness — explicit requests vs hints and hedging. (d) Interaction pattern — question-heavy, statement-heavy, or mixed. Classify each dimension as a spectrum value (0.0-1.0), not a binary.
ExtractSignals.step3: Extract values signals: what the user advocates for, defends, or expresses concern about. Categories: privacy, efficiency, creativity, social connection, independence, achievement, fairness, knowledge, health, family. Only register strong signals — avoid inferring values from ambiguous statements.
ExtractSignals.step4: Extract temporal patterns from turn_data.timestamp: hour-of-day bucket (morning/afternoon/evening/night), day-of-week. Track interaction frequency: time since last interaction. These patterns emerge over many turns — store raw data, let patterns emerge naturally. ADAPTATION EFFECTIVENESS: if turn_data contains adaptation_effectiveness metrics (from conversation.aisop.json AdaptResponse), extract and store: { style_match, emotional_alignment, proactive_relevance }. Track rolling average to measure personalization quality over time.
ExtractSignals.step5: SATISFACTION SIGNAL EXTRACTION: if turn_data contains adaptation_effectiveness metrics (from conversation.aisop.json AdaptResponse), extract satisfaction indicators. IMPLICIT SIGNALS from conversation_history: (a) topic_abandonment — user abruptly changes topic after AI response (satisfaction drop), (b) short_responses — user response length drops to < 5 words for 3+ consecutive turns after previously longer responses (disengagement), (c) re_asking — user rephrases same question within 3 turns (comprehension failure). EXPLICIT SIGNALS in user_input: 'that is not helpful', 'you do not understand', 'never mind', 'forget it', '没用', '你不懂', '算了'. Compute satisfaction_signal: float(0-1) where 1.0 = fully satisfied, 0.0 = clearly dissatisfied. Default 0.7 (mild positive) when no signal detected.
ExtractSignals.step5_5: FEEDBACK CORRECTION EXTRACTION: if turn_data contains correction_type='feedback_correction' or correction_type='user_correction', extract correction_rule: { corrected_aspect: string (from turn_data), old_value: string|null, new_value: string, applies_to: 'response_style'|'factual'|'preference' (classify based on corrected_aspect), correction_strength: 1.0, created_at: timestamp, decay_factor: 0.95 }. Pass correction_rule to UpdateProfile for storage. If turn_data does not contain correction_type, skip this step.
ExtractSignals.constraints: Lightweight extraction per turn — the profile is built incrementally, not from single-turn deep analysis. When in doubt, do NOT record a signal. False negatives (missing a signal) are far preferable to false positives (recording incorrect signals). Single turns should update the profile gently, not overwrite.
ExtractSignals.Error: Extraction fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: skip signal extraction for this turn, proceed to UpdateProfile with emotion data only -> inform caller.

InteractionMonitor.step1: COLLECT INTERACTION METRICS from current turn: (a) response_latency_category — estimate from timestamp gap between user message and agent response: 'fast' (<2s), 'normal' (2-10s), 'slow' (>10s). (b) turn_efficiency — ratio of useful content to total response length. Score 0-1: high (>0.7) means concise relevant response, low (<0.3) means verbose or off-topic. (c) user_engagement — derived from user message length and complexity relative to previous turns. Increasing length/complexity suggests engagement; decreasing suggests disengagement.
InteractionMonitor.step2: COMPUTE SATISFACTION PROXY: combine signals into a satisfaction estimate. satisfaction_proxy = 0.3*engagement_trend + 0.3*explicit_feedback_signal + 0.2*turn_efficiency + 0.2*response_relevance. Where engagement_trend is the slope of user message lengths over last 5 turns (positive=engaged), explicit_feedback_signal is from ExtractSignals satisfaction_signal, response_relevance is from adaptation_effectiveness.style_match if available.
InteractionMonitor.step3: QUALITY TREND ANALYSIS: compare current metrics against rolling averages. Detect: (a) QUALITY_IMPROVING — satisfaction_proxy trending up over 5+ turns. (b) QUALITY_DECLINING — satisfaction_proxy trending down over 3+ turns → flag for conversation.AdaptResponse repair check. (c) QUALITY_STABLE — no significant trend.
InteractionMonitor.step4: OUTPUT METRICS for observability: { interaction_metrics: { response_latency_category, turn_efficiency, user_engagement }, satisfaction_proxy: float(0-1), quality_trend: 'improving'|'declining'|'stable', quality_alert: boolean (true if declining for 3+ turns), metrics_timestamp: ISO_8601 }. Pass to UpdateProfile for storage in profile.interaction_quality section.
InteractionMonitor.constraints: Metrics are estimates, not ground truth — do not make high-stakes decisions based on single-turn metrics. Quality trend requires minimum 3 data points. satisfaction_proxy is a composite heuristic, not a direct measurement. Never expose raw metrics to user — they are internal observability data.
InteractionMonitor.Error: Metric collection fails -> fallback: skip interaction monitoring for this turn, pass through to UpdateProfile without metrics -> inform caller.

UpdateProfile.step1: Read existing profile from {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. If not found, initialize empty profile template: { schema_version: '1.0', user_id, created_at: now, updated_at: now, emotion_history: [], mood_baseline: { valence: 0, arousal: 0, stability: 1 }, preferences: { topics: {}, style: { formality: 0.5, verbosity: 0.5, directness: 0.5 }, interests: [], dislikes: [] }, habits: { active_times: {}, topic_patterns: {}, interaction_frequency: null }, cognitive_map: { values: {}, personality_signals: {}, communication_preferences: {} }, confidence_scores: {}, turn_count: 0, safety_flags: [], satisfaction_baseline: 0.7, satisfaction_trend: [], satisfaction_rolling_avg: null }.
UpdateProfile.step2: Merge new signals with existing profile using weighted averaging: newer signals weighted 1.5x relative to stored values. For topic preferences, increment frequency counters and update recency timestamps. For style dimensions, apply exponential moving average (alpha=0.3) to smooth single-turn noise. For values, only update when confidence > 0.6. Append emotion to emotion_history (cap at 200 entries, FIFO). Update mood_baseline. If adaptation_effectiveness data available, update adaptation_scores: { style_match_avg, emotional_alignment_avg, proactive_relevance_avg } using exponential moving average (alpha=0.2). If satisfaction_signal available from ExtractSignals, append to satisfaction_trend (cap at 50 entries, FIFO). Compute satisfaction_rolling_avg as exponential moving average (alpha=0.3). Update satisfaction_baseline using slower EMA (alpha=0.1) for long-term level. If cause_pattern data from TrackMood available, update cognitive_map.cause_correlations: { [cause_topic]: { frequency, avg_valence, last_seen } }. Increment turn_count. Set updated_at to current timestamp. FEEDBACK CORRECTIONS MERGE: if correction_rule data available from ExtractSignals, append to profile.feedback_corrections array (initialize as [] if absent). Apply decay to ALL existing corrections: multiply each correction.correction_strength by correction.decay_factor (default 0.95). Prune corrections with correction_strength < 0.1. Cap feedback_corrections array at 50 entries (FIFO). Active corrections (strength >= 0.1) are available for conversation.AdaptResponse personalization. If interaction_metrics available from InteractionMonitor, store in profile.interaction_quality: { latest_metrics: interaction_metrics, satisfaction_proxy_history: [append current, cap 50 FIFO], quality_trend: string, avg_satisfaction_proxy: EMA(alpha=0.3) }.
UpdateProfile.step3: Update confidence_scores for each profile section: confidence increases with more data points and consistent signals, decreases when signals are contradictory. Schema: { emotion_model: float, style_model: float, topics: float, values: float, habits: float }. Minimum 10 turns before any confidence exceeds 0.5.
UpdateProfile.step4: WRITE SAFETY: Write to {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json.tmp via file_system, then rename to {user_id}.json for atomic write. Before overwrite, copy existing to {user_id}.json.bak as backup. Verify written file size is non-zero.
UpdateProfile.constraints: DATA MINIMIZATION: only store aggregated signals, not raw user messages. Never store full conversation text in profiles. Profile JSON should not exceed 100KB — if approaching limit, prune oldest emotion_history entries. Respect user autonomy: profile is a tool for better service, not surveillance.
UpdateProfile.Error: Write fails -> retry with explicit directory creation via file_system -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return updated profile in response without persisting, flag persistence_failed: true -> inform caller.

LoadProfile.step1: Read {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. Validate schema: must contain user_id, created_at, updated_at, and at least one of emotion_history, preferences, habits, cognitive_map. If schema_version field is missing, set schema_version='1.0' (in-place migration).
LoadProfile.step2: If file not found, return empty profile template (same schema as UpdateProfile step1 initialization) with is_new: true flag. If file found but malformed, attempt partial recovery — extract valid fields, note corrupted sections.
LoadProfile.constraints: Read-only operation. Do NOT modify profile during query. Return profile with metadata: { profile, is_new: bool, data_age: string, turn_count: number }.
LoadProfile.Error: File read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return empty profile template with error note -> inform caller.

GenerateReport.step1: Read profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. If not found, return 'No profile data yet — we need more conversations to build your profile.' If found, synthesize into human-readable summary organized by category: (a) EMOTIONAL PATTERNS — dominant emotions, mood baseline (valence/arousal/stability in plain language), trajectory trends, any safety flags. (b) COMMUNICATION PREFERENCES — formality level, preferred verbosity, directness style, with examples of how to adapt. (c) INTERESTS & TOPICS — ranked by frequency and recency, with confidence levels. (d) HABITS — active time patterns, interaction frequency, topic recurrence patterns. (e) COGNITIVE MAP — detected values, personality signals, communication preferences.
GenerateReport.step2: Include confidence levels for each section: HIGH (>0.7, 20+ supporting data points), MEDIUM (0.4-0.7, 10-20 data points), LOW (<0.4, <10 data points). Mark LOW confidence sections with caveat: 'Based on limited interactions — may not be representative.' Include profile metadata: created_at, last updated, total turns analyzed.
GenerateReport.step3: TRANSPARENCY PRINCIPLE: the user has full right to see everything stored about them. The report must be a faithful representation of stored data — do not embellish, interpret beyond data, or omit. End with: 'This profile is based on [N] interactions. You can request a reset at any time.'
GenerateReport.constraints: Report is descriptive, not prescriptive. Never label the user ('you are an introvert') — describe observed patterns ('your messages tend toward concise, direct communication'). Use hedging language: 'tends to', 'often', 'appears to prefer'. No clinical or psychological diagnoses.
GenerateReport.Error: Report generation fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return raw profile data in structured format -> inform caller.

ConfirmReset.step1: Before clearing any data, generate a confirmation message: 'This will delete all learned preferences, emotional history, and behavioral patterns for user {user_id}. This action cannot be undone (though a backup will be retained temporarily). Please confirm: yes / no.'
ConfirmReset.step2: If confirmation received (explicit 'yes', 'confirm', or equivalent) -> proceed to ClearProfile. If declined or ambiguous -> abort, return { reset: 'cancelled', reason: 'User did not confirm' }.
ConfirmReset.constraints: REQUIRE explicit confirmation — do not proceed on ambiguous input. The right to deletion is absolute — if confirmed, execute without further delay or persuasion.
ConfirmReset.Error: Confirmation flow fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: abort reset, inform caller that reset requires explicit confirmation -> inform caller.

ClearProfile.step1: BACKUP: copy {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json to {user_id}.json.deleted_bak via file_system. Log deletion metadata: { user_id, deleted_at: timestamp, turn_count_at_deletion: N, reason: 'user_requested' }.
ClearProfile.step2: Delete {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. Also delete any related semantic data under {memory_dir}/semantic/ that references user_id. Verify deletion by attempting to read the file — should return not found.
ClearProfile.step3: Return confirmation: { reset: 'completed', user_id, deleted_at, backup_retained: true, message: 'All profile data has been cleared. A temporary backup exists for recovery within this session.' }.
ClearProfile.constraints: WRITE SAFETY: always backup before delete. Deletion must be thorough — no orphaned data fragments. The .deleted_bak file should be considered temporary and purged by system maintenance, not retained indefinitely.
ClearProfile.Error: Deletion fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: inform caller that deletion could not be completed, flag for manual review -> inform caller.

EditProfile.step1: Read {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json via file_system. If not found, initialize empty profile (same as UpdateProfile.step1). Validate edit_request: field_path must be a valid profile field (name, demographics.*, preferences.topics.*, preferences.style.*, interests, dislikes, habits.custom.*, cognitive_map.values.*). Reject system fields (schema_version, created_at, user_id, confidence_scores, emotion_history, turn_count, mood_baseline).
EditProfile.step2: APPLY EDIT: if operation='set', navigate to field_path in profile and set to new_value. Record old_value for confirmation. If field_path does not exist yet, create it. If operation='remove', delete the field at field_path. Update updated_at timestamp. Mark edit source as 'user_explicit' — these edits have maximum confidence (1.0) and override any learned values.
EditProfile.step3: WRITE SAFETY: Write to {memory_dir}/profiles/{user_id}.json.tmp, rename to {user_id}.json. Backup existing to {user_id}.json.bak. Log edit in profile audit trail: { edit_at, field_path, old_value, new_value, source: 'user_explicit' }.
EditProfile.step4: Return: { success: true, field_path, old_value, new_value, message: 'Profile updated successfully.' }.
EditProfile.constraints: User-explicit edits ALWAYS override learned values — the user is the authority on their own information. Rejected fields must be explained clearly. All edits logged for audit trail. Atomic write pattern mandatory.
EditProfile.Error: Read fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return {success: false, message: 'Could not access profile'} -> inform caller. Write fails -> retry -> circuit-breaker -> fallback: return edit result without persistence, flag persistence_failed: true -> inform caller.
